error.sessionExpired: Session expired. Please sign in again.

intro.title: Welcome to Shakh
intro.desc: I'm your coach. Together we'll start from your real level and build a tailored path, where I always explain the why behind the moves. It only takes a couple of minutes to get off to a good start.
intro.step1: 1 · A couple of quick questions about you
intro.step2: 2 · Link Lichess or Chess.com (if you want)
intro.step3: 3 · A mini-test to calibrate, and you're off

self.title: A few questions
self.desc: Go with your gut: it just helps us start from the right point.
self.rulesQ: Do you know how the pieces move?
self.experienceQ: How long have you been playing?
self.expSome: A few years, without training seriously
self.expExperienced: I've played for a while and I train

connect.title: Link an online account
connect.desc: Do you play on Lichess or Chess.com? Link it: after a quick verification we'll use your real rating to place you precisely and import your latest games. It's optional — you can also do it later from your profile.
connect.verified: Account verified: we've already placed you from your real rating.

test.title: Tactical mini-test
test.estimate: Provisional estimate: tactical rating ~{rating}, starting level {level}.
test.findBest: Find the best move. If you don't see it, you can skip.
test.verifiedNote: You already have a verified account: the test isn't required, but taking it refines the estimate further.

path.title: Your path
path.body: A leveled map takes you from beginner to club player. Nodes unlock once you prove you've mastered the previous ones.

coach.title: The coach explains the why
coach.body: No memorizing moves: the coach explains the meaning behind your choices, anchored to engine data and statistics.

free.title: Guided first, then free
free.body: At the start we tell you the next step. As you grow, the path stays as a reference and you train wherever you like.

done.title: All set
done.desc: I've placed you on the path and your dashboard is ready. From here on I'll guide you step by step. Good luck and enjoy the game!

landing.downloadStartedBody: Open Shakh-Setup.exe to install the app. Windows may warn you: “More info” → “Run anyway”.
landing.installDoneBody: Find Shakh among your apps — it opens full screen.
landing.installIosBody: Tap Share, then “Add to Home Screen”.
landing.installHintBody: Open your browser menu and pick “Install app” / “Add to Home Screen”.
landing.heroTitle: From your first move to club-level play.
landing.heroSub: Shakh takes you from beginner to a strong club player. An AI coach explains the why behind every move, anchored to engine data and real statistics.
landing.howSub: One method: study the why, don't memorize the what.
landing.featAnalysisBody: Import a game and review it move by move: the engine evaluates, the coach explains where it turned and why.
landing.featPathBody: A leveled map from beginner to club. Nodes unlock as you prove you've mastered the previous ones.
landing.featTheoryBody: Openings, endgames and middlegame to play on the board, not to read. Build and train your repertoire.
landing.featTacticsBody: Thousands of positions calibrated to your level, with spaced repetition on your weak themes.
landing.featTrapsBody: The traps you actually meet in your openings: how to set them and how to avoid them.
landing.coachTitle: A coach that explains the why.
landing.coachBody: No rote moves. The coach reasons over objective data — engine evaluation, Lichess statistics — and turns it into plain language: what the move threatens, why the best is best, what to train next.
landing.coachPoint1: Anchored to the Stockfish engine: zero hallucinated numbers.
landing.coachPoint3: Always available, on the position in front of you.
landing.finalSub: Create an account and take the diagnostic: three minutes to know where to start.

footer.tagline: The chess study platform with an AI coach.

consent.title: We respect your privacy
consent.body: We use technical cookies necessary for the platform to work and, only with your consent, preference cookies to remember your settings. No advertising tracking, no profiling.
consent.necessaryBody: Session, security and basic preferences. Always on, no consent required.
consent.preferencesBody: Remember non-essential choices (e.g. layout) to improve the experience.

empty.title: Start collecting data
empty.desc: Solve a few puzzles or import a game: the dashboard will fill up with progress, charts, and tailored advice.

nudge.title: Analyze your imported games
nudge.desc: You have {count} {count, plural, one {imported game} other {imported games}} still to analyze. Analyze them to unlock weaknesses, accuracy, and the coach's advice.

shakhRating.title: Shakh Rating
shakhRating.desc: A strength estimate on the real-world (OTB) scale, from puzzles, games, endgames, calculation, play quality, and — if linked — your online account's rating.
shakhRating.notCalibratedDetail: Estimate not yet calibrated: <mono>{collected}</mono>/<mono>{total}</mono> data points collected, <mono>{missing}</mono> still needed. Every puzzle solved, game analyzed, or endgame played counts.
shakhRating.refining: Estimate is being refined: you have enough data, keep training and the margin of error (±) will shrink.

skillsMap.title: Skills map
skillsMap.desc: Your profile across the five areas.

weaknesses.title: Priority weaknesses
weaknesses.noData: No data yet: at least {minAttempts} attempts on the same theme are needed for it to be evaluated.
weaknesses.noneFlagged: No weakness flagged: so far {attempts} attempts across {trackedThemes} {trackedThemes, plural, one {theme} other {themes}}, but at least {minAttempts} per theme are needed to flag one.

trend.needOne: 1 more {noun} needed to draw the trend.
trend.needMany: {missing} more {noun} needed to draw the trend.

distribution.empty: No errors recorded: great, or not much data yet.

glossary.title: Glossary of terms
glossary.desc: What the words you see in the dashboard and the analysis mean.

accuracy.def: How close your moves are to the engine's best ones, as a percentage. The higher it is, the better you played.

tacticalRating.def: A number measuring your strength in puzzles: it goes up when you solve them, down when you miss (like an Elo score).

streak.def: How many puzzles you've solved in a row without mistakes. The best is the longest streak you've reached.

pawn.def: The unit for measuring an advantage. 1 pawn ≈ having one extra minor piece, roughly. The engine measures it in hundredths (centipawns).

criticalArea.def: The phase of the game where you make the most mistakes: that's where it pays to train more.

opening.def: The first moves: you develop your pieces, control the center, and get your king to safety (castling).

middlegame.def: The heart of the game: plans, attacks, and combinations with many pieces still on the board.

endgame.def: The phase with few pieces left, where the pawns and the king take center stage.

fork.def: One piece attacks two targets at once: your opponent can only save one of them.

pin.def: A piece can't move because behind it sits a more valuable piece (often the king).

skewer.def: Like a pin in reverse: the valuable piece is in front and, when it moves, leaves the one behind it to be captured.

discovered.def: You move one piece and free another that gives check or attacks: two threats in one move.

sacrifice.def: You give up material on purpose to gain a bigger advantage (an attack on the king, mate, promotion).

castling.def: A special move that moves the king and rook together, to bring the king to safety.

development.def: Bringing your pieces (knights and bishops) out of their starting squares to active ones.

initiative.def: Having control of the game: you're the one creating threats and your opponent has to defend.

repertoire.def: The set of openings you choose to play and learn by heart.

games.headerDesc: Import, analyze, review move by move.
games.pageIntro: Import your games, analyze them with the engine, and review them move by move.
games.ledgerEyebrow: Your games · learn from every result
games.errNoValidGame: No valid game found in the PGN.
games.errPgnTooLarge: PGN too large (max ~500KB). Import fewer games at a time.
games.errLichessImport: Unexpected error while importing from Lichess.
games.errChesscomImport: Unexpected error while importing from Chess.com.
games.errCoachNotConfigured: The AI coach is not configured.
games.deleteConfirm: Delete this game and its analysis?
games.maxGamesTitle: Up to {max} games at a time
games.maxGamesDesc: Deselect a game to pick another.
games.analyzedInBackground: They're analyzed one at a time in the background.
games.emptyState: No games yet. Import one above to get started.
games.analyzeBgPlural: {n} games being analyzed in the background for your weak points.
games.analyzeBgSingular: 1 game being analyzed in the background for your weak points.
games.verifyTitle: Verify your account to make games count toward your profile.
games.verifyDesc: Only games from your verified account feed weak points, Shakh Rating, and statistics. You can still import other players' games: they stay browsable and analyzable, but don't touch your profile.
games.pgnLabel: One or more games in PGN format
games.fileUploadHint: Upload a .pgn file (multiple games are fine too).
games.lichessApiHint: Downloads the user's latest public games via the Lichess API.
games.chesscomApiHint: Downloads the user's most recent public games via the Chess.com API.
games.accuracyNote: The accuracy % is an estimate based on average centipawn loss, not the official Lichess/Chess.com standard.
games.legendIntro: The <bar>bar</bar> and the number in pawns tell you who's better: values with <plus>+</plus> favor White, with <minus>−</minus> Black. Each move is labeled like this:
games.engineAnalysisDesc: The analysis runs in parallel in your browser and keeps going even if you switch pages: every move is evaluated and classified.
games.depthExplain: It's how many half-moves ahead the engine calculates from each position before judging it. Higher = more reliable analysis, but slower (the time grows almost exponentially, not linearly).
games.depth12: 12 — fast, for a quick review or blitz games.
games.depth15: 15 — a balance of precision and time (recommended).
games.depth18: 18 — more precise (catches deep tactics and sacrifices), but can take 3–5× the time of 12.
games.depthAdvice: Below ~2000 Elo, 15 and 18 rarely change the verdict on big blunders: keep 15 by default, and go to 18 only for important games you want to study in depth.
games.otherGameRunning: Analysis running on another game: wait for it to finish.
games.analyzedOneAtATime: Analyzed one at a time in the background.
games.analyzeNGames: Analyze {count, plural, one {# game} other {# games}}

groups.indexDesc: Follow your students and assign activities, or join with a code.
groups.indexIntro: Create a club or a class to follow your students' progress and assign activities, or join a group with an invite code.
groups.noGroups: You're not part of any group yet. Create one or join with a code.
groups.createGroupDesc: You become the instructor of the group you create.
groups.joinWithCodeDesc: Got an invite? Paste the code here.
groups.membersDescOwner: Promote to instructor, remove, or open a student's progress.
groups.invitesDesc: Generate a code/link with a role and expiry. Each invite is valid for one entry.
groups.groupRepertoiresDesc: "This is how we play in our club." Students train them with the trainer.
groups.classDashboardIntro: {count, plural, one {# student} other {# students}}. Read-only aggregated view: every data access goes through the security checks.
groups.classEmptyState: No students in the group. Generate an invite to bring your students in.
groups.studentsDesc: Sort by column to see who's ahead and who's behind.
groups.avgProficiencyDesc: Average profile across the five areas.
groups.commonWeaknessesDesc: The themes where more students fall below the threshold.
groups.classSummaryDesc: Coach summary of the aggregated data.
groups.assignmentsIntro: Assign activities to a student or the whole class. Completion is derived from real progress; where that's not possible, the student marks it done.
groups.newAssignmentDesc: Lesson, puzzle, endgame, trap, repertoire, or node.
groups.drilldownIntro: Teaching context: the student's progress as they see it, read-only.
groups.drilldownEmptyState: This student doesn't have enough data for the dashboard yet.
groups.errPerm: You don't have permission for this action.
groups.errOwnerNotAssignable: The owner role can't be assigned here.
groups.errStudentNotInGroup: The student isn't part of this group.
groups.emailReservePlaceholder: reserve the invite for an email
groups.clickwrapNotice: By joining the group, the instructor will be able to see your progress (read-only) to help you improve. You can leave the group at any time.
groups.assignedByInstructorDesc: Activities to complete, with due dates and status.
groups.inProgressCount: {count, plural, one {# in progress} other {# in progress}}
groups.synthesisPlaceholder: Generate a summary of the class's aggregated weaknesses, with a lesson suggestion. The data is deterministic; the coach only writes the summary.

list.title: Unforgettable games
list.subtitle: The most celebrated games in chess history, move by move, with the story of what makes them immortal.

viewer.keyboardHint: Tip: navigate with the ← → arrow keys.

hub.title: Play
hub.desc: Same device or asynchronous online with a shareable link.
hub.heading: Play a friend
hub.lead: Challenge a friend on the same device, or play asynchronously online with a shareable link. Pick the time control, review the moves, step back.

quickMatch.title: Find an opponent
quickMatch.desc: Get paired with a player from anywhere in the world. Pick a time control and go.

notFound.title: Game not found
notFound.desc: The game doesn't exist, is already full, or you don't have access.

error.signInToPlay: You must sign in to play.
error.signInToAnalyze: You must sign in to analyze a game.
error.notPlayerInGame: You are not a player in this game.

resign.label: Resign
resign.confirmDesc: You'll lose the game. This action can't be undone.

create.title: New online game
create.desc: Create the game, share the link with a friend, and play whenever you like: moves sync in real time.

myGames.title: Your online games
myGames.empty: No online games yet. Create one to get started.

hotseat.setupDesc: Take turns on the same screen. The board can flip automatically to the side that's to move.
hotseat.autoFlip: Flip the board on every move

waiting.shareLink: Share this link with your friend. They'll need to sign in to play.
waiting.invitedAs: You've been invited to play. Join as {color}.

sparring.desc: Full games against a personality and a strength you choose.
sparring.lead: Play full games against the engine with a personality and a strength you choose: aggressive, positional, or solid. Drill your openings and sharpen your play against different styles.

coach.title: Coach
coach.observing: The coach is watching your move…
coach.empty: Play a move: the coach will tell you what it changes on the board.

profile.profileCardDesc: How we present you in the app.
profile.appearanceDesc: The theme applies right away; save to remember it.
profile.groupsDesc: Join a club or a class, or create your own.
profile.privacyDesc: Export your data or permanently delete your account.
profile.exportMyDataDesc: Profile, games, progress, and repertoires in JSON format.
profile.deleteAccountDesc: Permanent removal of your account and linked data. Irreversible.
profile.deleteDialogDesc: This action is irreversible: your profile, games, progress, and repertoires will be deleted forever.
profile.verifyHintLichess: Paste the token into your Lichess bio (Preferences → Edit profile → Biography), save, then press Verify.
profile.verifyHintChesscom: Paste the token into the Name or Location field of your Chess.com profile (Settings → Profile), save, then press Verify.
profile.externalDesc: Link Lichess or Chess.com: after verification, your online rating weighs heavily on your Shakh Rating, mapped to the real-world (OTB) scale.
profile.tokenGeneratedDesc: Add it to your profile, then verify.
profile.errSessionExpired: Session expired. Please sign in again.
profile.errTokenExpired: Token expired. Relink the account to generate a new one.
profile.errTokenNotFound: Token not found in your profile. Save the token, wait a few seconds, then try again.

coach.title: Coach
coach.desc: A summary of your recurring weaknesses from your game data.

intro.before: The coach analyzes your game data (computed by the engine) and summarizes your recurring weaknesses. For move-by-move explanations, open a game from the

noData.title: No data yet
noData.desc: Import and analyze a few games: the coach's summary is based on the errors detected by the engine.

errorsByPhase.title: Your errors by phase
errorsByPhase.desc: Across {games} games, {moves} of your moves examined.

summary.title: Coach summary
summary.desc: A motivating, actionable recap of your error patterns.

coach.notConfiguredInline: The AI coach isn't configured (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is missing).

move.analyzeFirst: Analyze the game first with the engine: the coach explains the data the engine computes.
move.selectMove: Select a move to have it explained.
move.label: Move
move.basedOnEngine: Explanations are based on the engine's data (evaluations and best move), they don't recompute it.

toast.noNewErrors: No new errors to comment on

synthesis.analyzing: The coach is analyzing your patterns…
synthesis.notConfigured: The AI coach isn't configured (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is missing). The metrics above remain available.

chat.empty: Ask something about the position, e.g. "what's the plan for White?" or "why not Nd4?".

weaknesses.title: Weaknesses
weaknesses.desc: The losses that repeat, grouped and sorted by severity.
weaknesses.intro: Not single mistakes, but the losses that repeat across your games. The engine groups them, sorts them by severity, and points you to where to train.

empty.base: You need at least {min} analyzed games to detect reliable patterns. You have {analyzed}
empty.pendingSuffix: , but {pending} imported are still waiting to be analyzed.

weaknesses.noPatterns: No recurring weaknesses stand out in your recent games. Keep it up — analyze more games to sharpen the picture.

path.title: Path
path.desc: Nodes unlock as you master the previous ones.
path.intro: From beginner to club player. Nodes unlock as you prove you've mastered the previous ones.
path.curriculumNote: The curriculum is an instructional-design draft: a starting point, not a definitive truth.

nextStep.title: Next step
nextStep.completeDiagnostic: Complete the diagnostic to get a personalized recommendation.

skillTree.unlockHint: Unlock by completing the previous steps.

repertoire.title: Repertoire
repertoire.desc: Build lines by color, then drill them with spaced repetition.
repertoire.intro: Build your lines by color, then drill them with spaced repetition. Add moves by playing on the board or from the explorer.
repertoire.emptyEditor: Empty repertoire: add some lines in the editor first.

error.sessionExpiredSignIn: Session expired. Please sign in again.
error.noDataYet: Analyze a few games first: there's no data yet.
error.coachNotConfigured: The AI coach isn't configured (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is missing).
error.coachUnparsable: Coach response couldn't be parsed. Try again.

tactics.title: Tactics
tactics.hubIntro: Train your tactical vision with puzzles. The rating adapts to you and missed puzzles come back for review.

adaptive.title: Adaptive
adaptive.desc: A continuous flow of puzzles at your level. Updates rating and streak.

theme.title: By theme
theme.desc: Train a specific motif: fork, pin, endgames…

timed.title: Timed challenge
timed.desc: 3 minutes, rising difficulty: how many can you solve?

tactics.reviewEmpty: You have no due puzzles. Come back later or train in adaptive mode.
tactics.noPuzzlesForCriteria: No puzzles found for these criteria. Import the dataset or change mode.
tactics.sessionExpired: Session expired. Please sign in again.
tactics.noSuitablePuzzle: No suitable puzzle found. Import the puzzle dataset or try again later.
tactics.blindfoldIntro: Memorize the position, then the board clears: play the entire combination from memory, without seeing the pieces. The opponent replies in your head — we just confirm it in words.
tactics.depthHint: Depth rises when you solve, drops when you miss.

home.title: Theory
home.mobileDesc: Understand the why of moves, with real data.
home.desc: Guided lessons over the board: understand the why of moves, with real explorer data and the exact truth of endgames.

learn.title: Learn
learn.mobileDesc: Understand the idea, recognize it, exploit it. Then explained games.
learn.desc: The fundamentals, one concept at a time. First you understand the idea, then you recognize it and exploit it in small exercises. Then watch games explained move by move.

openings.title: Openings
openings.mobileDesc: Families, openings and variations. Open a lesson to study it.
openings.desc: Browse the ECO tree: families, openings and variations. Open a lesson to study it on the board.

middlegame.title: Middlegame
middlegame.mobileDesc: Plans for both sides and “find the plan” exercises.
middlegame.desc: Strategic themes on typical positions: plans for both sides and "find the plan" exercises, evaluated by the engine and commented by the coach.

endgames.title: Endgames
endgames.mobileDesc: Convert the result against the tablebase's perfect defense.
endgames.desc: Endgame technique, lesson and practice: convert the result against the tablebase's perfect defense. No approximations — the tablebase is absolute truth. Every conversion updates your endgame rating.

errors.sessionExpired: Session expired. Please sign in again.

deviation.notConfigured: Coach not configured: you can still evaluate the deviation with the engine.

openingTree.empty: No published openings. Content comes with the seeds.
openingTree.resultsCapped: Showing the first {count} matches — refine your search.

tablebase.title: Exact endgame (tablebase)
tablebase.tooManyPieces: The evaluation here is the engine's (see the Engine panel).

repertoireEditor.deleteMoveTitle: Delete this move and its continuation
repertoireEditor.selectMove: Select a move to annotate it.

repertoireList.name: Name
repertoireList.empty: No repertoires. Create one to start building your lines.

endgamePractice.title: Endgame practice
endgamePractice.throwsAwayWin: That throws away the win: the position is now {quality}. Go back and try again.
endgamePractice.losesDraw: Careful: this move loses the draw (position {quality}). Go back and try again.
endgamePractice.stalemateWin: Stalemate: the win slipped into a draw.
endgamePractice.endedDraw: Ended in a draw: the win slipped away.
endgamePractice.drawHeld: Draw held: you proved the defense. Result confirmed by the tablebase.
endgamePractice.notSafeDraw: The position isn't a safe draw yet: keep defending.
endgamePractice.disclaimer: The opponent plays the Lichess tablebase's perfect defense. After each of your moves the result is verified: no approximations.

lessonViewer.deviationText: You're exploring a move outside the lesson. Evaluate it with the engine or ask the coach why it isn't the best choice.
lessonViewer.lessonCompleted: Lesson completed — recorded in your path.
lessonViewer.navigateExplore: Navigate the moves or play on the board to explore.
lessonViewer.engineOff: Engine off. Turn it on to evaluate the position and deviations.

openingTrainer.leftRepertoire: The opponent left your repertoire with {move} (the most common move). Consider adding it.
openingTrainer.endOfLine: End of the line covered by the repertoire.

positionalExercise.engineAnalyzing: The engine is analyzing the position…
positionalExercise.coachUnavailable: The coach isn't available right now; rely on the engine's evaluation.
positionalExercise.couldNotEvaluate: I couldn't evaluate the move. Try again.
positionalExercise.title: Exercise: find the plan
positionalExercise.proposeMove: Propose a move on the board: the engine evaluates it and the coach explains whether it fits the plan.
positionalExercise.disclaimer: There's no single solution: what's evaluated is the reasonableness of the plan. The engine gives the numbers, the coach the words.

conceptRunner.noExercises: No exercises available right now for this concept.
conceptRunner.solvedSummary: You solved <solved></solved> of {total} cleanly. Concept <title></title> unlocked.
